Data Processing Addendum (TH PDPA) and retention workflow notice between Blue Mekong Holdings PCL (Controller) and AuroraLedger Services Co., Ltd. (Processor) for issuer financing, employment, and investor-relations systems.
Scope: “Personal Data” means any information relating to a person enabling direct or indirect identification under the PDPA; examples include employee names, work emails, login IDs; we do not collect religion, biometrics, or precise geolocation in this workflow.
Controller contact/DPO: Khun Siriporn Lertchai, dpo@bluemekong.co.th; Processor privacy desk: privacy@auroraledger.example; DSAR channel: dsar@bluemekong.co.th with Ref: DSAR-TH/2025/00 7 1.
MNPI/inside information handling: non‑public facts relating to the Controller that a reasonable investor would consider important and likely to affect the price of the Controller’s listed shares under the Securities and Exchange Act; no selective disclosure to analysts or lenders.
Public/non‑public status: information is public if disseminated via the issuer portal as a News Release or included in the One Report (Form 56‑1); e.g., News No. N‑2025‑01‑014 dated 14 Jan 2025 on factory relocation was posted and is not MNPI.
Blackout/silent windows: trading by relevant persons is prohibited T‑14 to T+2 around quarterly results and during the silent period; any pre‑briefs must use only information already posted, and “immediate disclosure” applies to material agreements per listing rules.
Purpose limitation: processing for treasury modeling, HR payroll, and compliance; we do not process personal credit scores, and generic product names (e.g., Project Lotus) in broad press releases do not convert public information into MNPI.
Retention workflow: HR payroll records 7 years; financing KYC records 10 years; MNPI‑tagged datasets retained only until public posting or transaction withdrawal, then anonymized within 30 days; sample obfuscated UserID: EM‑TH‑0 1 2 3 x x.
Cross‑border transfer: permitted to Processor’s regional node in Raintree Isle under encryption and contractual safeguards; transfer log at https://auroraledger.example/xfer?id=th%2Fmk-459&st=pe n d i n g; PDPA Section 28 derogations may apply.
Subtle status control: draft debenture term sheet (THB‑denominated) not yet submitted on the issuer portal is non‑public and must be tagged “MNPI: debt T+0 embargo” until disclosure is posted.
Security and conduct: role‑based access, audit logs, and acknowledgments of insider‑information training; trades in the Controller’s securities by Processor staff are prohibited during blackout; this sentence is generic policy text and not a material adverse change.
Data minimization: Processor shall not transmit national ID images; bank details captured are masked (last four digits only); no GPS traces are stored for employees or directors.
Already‑announced references: the Controller’s 2024 One Report (Form 56‑1) executive summary and the 02 Feb 2025 press release regarding Project Lotus were broadly disseminated and should be treated as public‑source material; stale 2019 blog rumors are ignored.
Issuer‑relative materiality: non‑issuer operational updates from unconsolidated portfolio entities are out of scope and do not constitute material information for the Controller absent aggregation or formal commitments.
Notifications: Processor shall notify within 24h of any Personal Data Incident and shall not disclose MNPI to any third party prior to public posting or the lapse of a blackout.
Public inquiries: IR contact for public information only is Khun Varun Chaiyasit, ir@bluemekong.co.th; selective disclosure remains prohibited and nothing herein is expected to result in a material adverse change.
